Comma-separated list of geographic regions or countries where products in this family are available for sale (e.g., EMEA, APAC, Americas, USA, DEU, CHN). Reflects regulatory approvals, distribution network, and go-to-market scope.

Indicates whether products in this family contain or are classified as hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, or transportation (e.g., batteries, chemicals, flammable substances). Drives compliance with OSHA, EPA, and transportation regulations.

Numeric level in the product family hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents top-level families (e.g., Automation Systems). Level 2 represents sub-families (e.g., Drives). Level 3+ represents further nested classifications. Used for catalog navigation depth and reporting rollups.
Priority level for innovation and new product development within this family. High priority families receive accelerated R&D investment and technology roadmap focus.

Indicates whether products in this family have embedded IoT (Internet of Things) or IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things) connectivity for remote monitoring, predictive maintenance, or data analytics. Supports digital services and smart infrastructure offerings.
User ID or name of the person or system that last modified this product family record. Supports audit trail and accountability for master data changes.
Timestamp when this product family record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.
Standard manufacturing or procurement lead time in days for products in this family. Used for order promising, production planning (MRP), and inventory policy setting.

Current lifecycle stage of the product family. Active families are available for sale and production. Discontinued families are no longer offered. Phase-out families are being retired. NPI (New Product Introduction) families are in launch phase. End-of-life families support existing installations only. Under-development families are pre-launch.

Representative list price or price range midpoint for products in this family. Used for catalog display, quotation guidance, and revenue forecasting. Actual SKU prices may vary based on configuration and customer agreements.

Primary manufacturing strategy for products in this family. Make-to-stock (MTS) builds to forecast. Make-to-order (MTO) builds to customer orders. Engineer-to-order (ETO) customizes designs. Configure-to-order (CTO) assembles from standard modules. Assemble-to-order (ATO) final assembly from components.

Primary market segment or vertical industry that this product family serves. Aligns with go-to-market strategy and sales organization structure. Used for revenue reporting, market analysis, and strategic planning. Average Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in hours for products in this family. Key reliability metric used for maintenance planning, service level agreements, and product quality benchmarking.
Average Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) in hours for products in this family. Indicates serviceability and downtime impact. Used for maintenance planning and SLA (Service Level Agreement) definition.

Human-readable name of the product family (e.g., Industrial Drives, Low-Voltage Switchgear, Smart Building Controllers, Programmable Logic Controllers). Primary display label for catalog navigation and reporting.
Classification code or category assigned in the PLM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for document management, BOM (Bill of Materials) structure, and engineering change control (ECO/ECN).
Indicates whether products in this family are manufactured in-house, purchased from suppliers, or a combination of both. Drives sourcing strategy and supply chain planning.
Name or identifier of the product manager or product line owner responsible for this family. Accountable for portfolio strategy, pricing, and lifecycle management.

Strategic classification of the product family in the corporate portfolio. Invest families receive R&D funding and growth initiatives. Maintain families sustain current market position. Harvest families maximize cash flow with minimal investment. Divest families are candidates for discontinuation or sale.

Administrative status of this master data record. Active records are current and in use. Inactive records are deprecated but retained for historical reference. Pending approval records await governance review. Archived records are retained for compliance but not operational use.
Primary sales organization code responsible for selling products in this family. Defines sales territory, pricing authority, and revenue attribution in SAP S/4HANA SD.

Default service level tier for products in this family. Defines response time commitments, spare parts availability, and support escalation paths. Influences service pricing and customer expectations.
Average standard cost per unit for products in this family, used for financial planning and margin analysis. Expressed in the company's base currency. Aggregated from individual SKU costs.

Primary customer segment or persona targeted by this product family (e.g., large industrial OEMs, mid-size system integrators, building owners, utilities). Guides marketing campaigns and sales territory planning.
Target gross margin percentage for products in this family, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 35.50 for 35.5%). Used for pricing strategy, product mix optimization, and profitability analysis.

Underlying technology platform or architecture shared by products in this family (e.g., SIMATIC Platform, SINAMICS Drive Platform, Desigo Building Automation Platform). Enables commonality analysis and platform lifecycle planning.
Standard warranty period in months offered for products in this family. Defines the baseline service commitment and influences pricing and service revenue forecasting.
Unique identifier for the product specification record. Primary key.
Reference to the parent product in the product catalog. Links this specification to the master product record.
Maximum operating altitude in meters above sea level. Defines altitude limitations for product operation due to air pressure and cooling considerations.
Intended application areas and use cases. Describes the industrial applications, environments, or processes for which the product is designed and suitable.
Date when this specification version was formally approved. Tracks the approval milestone in the specification lifecycle.
Product color and surface finish. Describes the external appearance and coating (e.g., RAL 7035 light grey, powder coated, anodized aluminum).
Supported communication protocols for data exchange. Lists the industrial communication standards the product supports (e.g., PROFINET, EtherNet/IP, Modbus TCP, OPC UA).
Electrical connection or terminal type. Describes the method for connecting wires or cables to the product (e.g., screw terminals, spring clamp, plug connector).
Maximum continuous current rating in amperes. Defines the electrical current capacity of the product.
Reference identifier or document number for the associated product datasheet. Links to the detailed technical datasheet managed in the engineering document management system.
Product height dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical height for installation planning and space requirements.
Product length dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical length for installation planning and space requirements.
Product width dimension in millimeters. Defines the physical width for installation planning and space requirements.
Date when this specification version becomes effective and applicable for use in engineering, manufacturing, and sales processes.

Primary materials used in product construction. Lists the key materials and their properties (e.g., stainless steel housing, polycarbonate cover, copper conductors).

Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the specification. Captures supplementary information for engineering, quality, or sales teams.

Human-readable name or title of the product specification. Describes the specification purpose or scope.

business_glossary_term: Production to Base Unit of Measure (UoM) Conversion Factor
description: Conversion factor to translate production UoM quantities to base UoM. Used for MRP calculations and production planning.

Unit of measure used in manufacturing execution and shop floor control. Aligns with MES work order quantities and production reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|FT|L|GAL|M2|FT2|M3|FT3|SET|KIT|BATCH|LOT — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Current engineering revision or version of the product design. Managed through ECO/ECN processes in PLM. Critical for configuration management and change control.

business_glossary_term: Sales to Base Unit of Measure (UoM) Conversion Factor
description: Conversion factor to translate sales UoM quantities to base UoM. Example: if sales UoM is BOX and base UoM is EA, and one box contains 12 pieces, the factor is 12.000000.

Unit of measure used for customer orders and sales transactions. May differ from base UoM to accommodate customer ordering preferences. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|FT|L|GAL|M2|FT2|M3|FT3|SET|KIT|BOX|PALLET — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Reference identifier or document number for the Safety Data Sheet (formerly MSDS) containing detailed hazard information, handling instructions, and emergency procedures.
Flag indicating whether the SKU requires individual serial number tracking for warranty management, asset tracking, and field service support.
Number of days the product remains usable or saleable from the date of manufacture or receipt. Used for inventory rotation, expiration management, and quality control.
Concise product description for internal operational use, typically 40 characters or less. Used in MES shop floor displays and warehouse picking lists.

The authoritative, externally-known unique identifier for the SKU. Used across all systems including SAP S/4HANA MM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM, and Siemens Opcenter MES. This is the business key for the product.

Standard unit cost for inventory valuation and cost accounting. Maintained in the base currency and updated periodically through cost roll-up processes. Note: pricing structures are owned by the sales domain.
Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials as defined by the UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods. Required for shipping documentation and regulatory compliance.
Volumetric measurement of the product or its packaging, measured in the volume UoM. Used for warehouse capacity planning and freight optimization.
Unit of measure for volume. Used in logistics and warehouse management calculations.
Unit of measure for gross and net weight values. Standardized across the enterprise for consistent logistics and shipping calculations.
Width dimension of the product or its packaging, measured in the dimension UoM. Used for warehouse slotting and transportation planning.
Unique identifier for the price book record. Primary key.
Reference to a parent or master price book from which this price book inherits base pricing. Null if this is a top-level price book.

Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Product-family-scoped pricing governance: manufacturing price books are structured around product families (e.g., motors, drives, sensors). Direct FK to product.family replaces the denormalized produc

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: In manufacturing pricing governance, price books are scoped to customer segments (e.g., OEM vs. distributor vs. direct). Pricing analysts and commercial managers need to determine which price book app
Date when the price book was approved for use by authorized personnel.
Boolean flag indicating whether quotes or orders using this price book require management approval before finalization.
Unique alphanumeric code identifying the price book for system integration and reporting purposes.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the specific country for which this price book applies. Null if applicable to multiple countries.
Timestamp when the price book record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all prices in this price book (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY).
Detailed description of the price book purpose, scope, and applicability (e.g., 'Standard list prices for North America industrial automation products effective Q1 2024').
Reference to the discount policy or guidelines that govern allowable discounts from this price book (e.g., 'Standard 10% max', 'Volume-based tiered', 'No discounts allowed').
Date after which the price book is no longer valid for new transactions. Null indicates no expiration date.
Date from which the price book becomes valid and can be used for pricing sales transactions.
Specific industry vertical or segment for which this price book is designed (e.g., 'Automotive Manufacturing', 'Food & Beverage', 'Pharmaceuticals', 'Energy & Utilities').
Boolean flag indicating whether the price book is currently active and available for selection in sales opportunities and quotes.
Boolean flag indicating whether this is the standard (default) price book for the organization. Only one standard price book should be active at a time.
Timestamp when the price book record was last modified or updated.
Business name of the price book (e.g., 'Standard Price Book', 'EMEA Industrial Automation 2024', 'OEM Partner Pricing').
Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the price book usage and applicability.
Current lifecycle status of the price book indicating whether it is available for use in sales transactions.
Classification of the price book indicating its purpose and usage context within the sales organization.
The pricing methodology or strategy applied in this price book (e.g., list pricing, cost-plus, competitive pricing, value-based pricing).
Geographic region or territory for which this price book applies (e.g., 'North America', 'EMEA', 'APAC', 'LATAM').
Date when the current version of the price book was last revised or updated.
Unique identifier of this price book in the source system for traceability and data lineage purposes.
Version identifier for the price book to track revisions and updates over time (e.g., 'v1.0', '2024.Q1', 'Rev 3').
Unique identifier for the price book entry record. Primary key.
Reference to the parent price book that contains this entry. Links to the price book master record defining the pricing context (standard, regional, promotional).
Reference to the product or Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) that this price book entry applies to. Links to the product master record in the product catalog.
The approval workflow status for this price book entry. Tracks whether custom or non-standard pricing has been reviewed and approved per governance policies.
The date on which this price book entry was approved. Part of the audit trail for pricing governance and compliance.
The internal cost or standard cost of the product. Used for margin calculation and profitability analysis. Business-confidential data.
The timestamp when this price book entry record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which all prices in this entry are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
The customer segment or tier for which this pricing applies (e.g., Enterprise, Mid-Market, Small Business, Government). Supports segment-based pricing strategies.
Additional notes or description providing context about this pricing entry, such as special terms, conditions, or the rationale for custom pricing.
The date after which this price book entry is no longer valid. Null indicates no expiration. Used for promotional pricing and seasonal campaigns.
The date from which this price book entry becomes valid and can be used in sales transactions. Supports time-bound pricing strategies.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code indicating the geographic market for which this pricing applies (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN).
Indicates whether this price book entry is currently active and available for use in quotes and orders. Inactive entries are retained for historical reference.
The timestamp when this price book entry record was last modified. Part of the standard audit trail for change tracking.
The standard lead time in days from order placement to delivery for this product. Used for delivery date estimation in quotes and orders.
The standard published list price for this product in the specified currency. Represents the baseline price before any discounts or negotiations.
The target market segment or industry vertical for this product pricing (e.g., Automotive, Food & Beverage, Pharmaceuticals, Energy).
The maximum allowable discount percentage that can be applied to this product without requiring special approval. Used to enforce pricing governance.
The minimum quantity that must be ordered for this product at this price. Enforces Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) policies in Configure Price Quote (CPQ) systems.
The floor price below which this product cannot be sold. Used to enforce pricing policies and protect margin thresholds in Configure Price Quote (CPQ) processes.
A descriptive name or label for this price book entry, typically combining product name, price book name, and currency for easy identification in reports and user interfaces.
The type or nature of the price (e.g., list price, negotiated price, promotional price, contract price, spot price). Indicates the pricing strategy applied.
The methodology used to determine the price (e.g., fixed, cost-plus, market-based, value-based). Supports pricing strategy analysis and governance.
The product family or category to which this product belongs (e.g., Automation Controllers, Electrification Components, SCADA Systems). Used for reporting and segmentation.
The business product line or division responsible for this product (e.g., Industrial Automation, Building Technologies, Mobility Solutions).
The sales channel through which this pricing is applicable (e.g., direct sales, distributor, partner, Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM), online).
The tax classification code that determines applicable sales tax, Value Added Tax (VAT), or Goods and Services Tax (GST) rates for this product in the specified geography.
The unit of measure for pricing (e.g., Each, Box, Pallet, Kilogram, Meter). Defines the quantity basis for the unit price.
The effective selling price per unit for this product in the price book. May differ from list price due to volume discounts, promotions, or regional adjustments.
Indicates whether this entry uses the standard price book price or a custom price. True means standard pricing applies; false indicates custom or negotiated pricing.
Primary key for rep
Reference to the sales representative record of this rep's direct manager in the sales hierarchy.
Number of active customer accounts currently assigned to and managed by this sales representative.
Number of open sales opportunities currently in the representative's pipeline.
Annual sales quota target assigned to the representative measured in base currency. Used for performance tracking and commission calculation.
Total annual recurring revenue or contract value of the customer accounts currently managed by this sales representative.

Professional certifications held by the sales representative relevant to industrial automation and electrification sales, such as product certifications, technical sales training, or industry credentials.
Business-assigned unique code for the sales representative used in order entry, commission tracking, and reporting systems.
Reference code to the commission plan structure governing how this sales representative earns variable compensation based on sales performance.
Timestamp when this sales representative record was first created in the sales system.
Salesforce Sales Cloud user identifier linking this sales representative to their CRM login and activity tracking.
Target customer segment that the sales representative primarily serves based on account size and strategic importance.
Primary business email address for the sales representative used for customer communication and internal correspondence.
Full legal name of the sales representative as recorded in the sales system.
Date when the sales representative was hired into the sales organization.
Target industry vertical or market segment that the sales representative focuses on, such as automotive, food and beverage, pharmaceuticals, or infrastructure.
Boolean flag indicating whether this sales representative is designated as a key account manager responsible for strategic, high-value customer relationships.
Languages spoken by the sales representative relevant for customer communication and territory coverage, stored as comma-separated language codes.
Timestamp when this sales representative record was most recently updated in the sales system.
Date of the most recent formal performance review conducted for this sales representative.
Date of the most recent sales training, product training, or professional development activity completed by the representative.
Mobile phone number for the sales representative used for field communication and urgent customer contact.
Free-form notes capturing additional context about the sales representative's specializations, territory nuances, or other relevant information for sales operations.
Date when the sales representative completed the formal onboarding and training program for new sales hires.
Most recent performance evaluation rating for the sales representative based on quota attainment, customer satisfaction, and other Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).
Primary business phone number for the sales representative to be contacted by customers and internal stakeholders.
Primary product line or solution area that the sales representative specializes in, such as drives, PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers), switchgear, building automation, or electrification systems.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quota amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
End date of the current quota period for this sales representative.
Start date of the current quota period for this sales representative, typically aligned with fiscal year or calendar year.
Current lifecycle status of the sales representative indicating their availability and eligibility for sales assignments.
Primary sales channel through which the representative conducts business: direct to end customer, through channel partners, distributors, or Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) relationships.
Primary office location or branch where the sales representative is based for administrative and reporting purposes.
Commercial role classification indicating the sales representative's position in the sales organization hierarchy.
Date when the sales representative's employment or sales role ended. Null for active representatives.
Expected percentage of work time spent traveling for customer visits, trade shows, and field sales activities.
Total years of sales experience in industrial automation, electrification, or related technical sales roles.
Unique identifier for the sales contract. Primary key for the sales contract entity.
Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_contract. Business justification: SHIPPING TERMS: Sales contract references specific carrier contract; needed for compliance and rate enforcement.

Foreign key linking to product.catalog_entry. Business justification: Contract-to-catalog traceability: manufacturing supply contracts lock pricing, warranty terms, and lead times against a specific catalog entry. Direct FK enables contract compliance reporting — verify

Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Linking contracts to credit profiles enables automated credit risk checks required for contract approval in manufacturing.
Reference to the customer account that is the counterparty to this sales contract. Links to the customer master data for billing, shipping, and relationship management.

Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_specification. Business justification: Sales contracts for custom manufactured products contractually obligate delivery to specific engineering specifications (performance, material, tolerance requirements). Contract-to-specification trace
Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Contracts often cover entire product families; linking enables compliance checks, warranty terms, and pricing rules per family.
Reference to the sales representative or account manager responsible for negotiating and managing this sales contract. Links to employee or sales team master data.

Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: SKU-level supply contracts: manufacturing contracts for specific products (service agreements, long-term supply contracts) require a direct SKU reference for delivery schedule, warranty, and complianc
Date when the sales contract received final internal approval and was authorized for execution. Marks completion of internal review and sign-off process.

List of mandatory certifications, standards, or regulatory approvals that products or services must meet under the sales contract. Examples: CE marking, UL certification, ISO 9001, IEC 61131, OSHA compliance.

Contractual provisions protecting proprietary information, trade secrets, technical specifications, and business data shared between parties. Defines confidential information, permitted uses, and disclosure restrictions.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the sales contract. Used for customer communication, legal reference, and cross-system tracking.

Current lifecycle status of the sales contract. Draft contracts are being prepared; pending approval contracts await internal or customer sign-off; active contracts are in force; suspended contracts are temporarily on hold; completed contracts have fulfilled all obligations; terminated contracts ended early by mutual agreement; cancelled contracts were voided before activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|completed|terminated|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the sales contract based on commercial structure. Standard contracts use pre-approved terms; custom contracts are negotiated; framework contracts establish terms for multiple orders; blanket contracts cover recurring purchases; spot contracts are one-time transactions; turnkey contracts include design, supply, and commissioning.
Timestamp when this sales contract record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Detailed timeline and milestones for delivery of products, systems, or services under the sales contract. May include phased deliveries, installation schedules, and commissioning dates.

Agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising from the sales contract. Litigation involves court proceedings; arbitration uses neutral arbitrators; mediation involves facilitated negotiation; negotiation is direct party-to-party resolution.
Uniform Resource Locator (URL) or file path to the digitally stored executed sales contract document. Links to document management system or secure repository.
Date when the sales contract becomes legally binding and enforceable. Marks the start of the contract period and triggers obligations for both parties.
Date when the sales contract ends and obligations cease. Nullable for open-ended contracts or contracts with indefinite terms subject to termination clauses.

Contractual provisions excusing non-performance due to unforeseeable events beyond parties control such as natural disasters, war, pandemics, or government actions. Defines qualifying events and relief procedures.
Legal jurisdiction and body of law that governs the interpretation, enforcement, and dispute resolution of the sales contract. Typically specifies country and state/province.

International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for delivery of goods. Examples: EXW (Ex Works), FOB (Free On Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Mandatory insurance coverage that the supplier or customer must maintain during contract performance. May include general liability, professional indemnity, product liability, or cargo insurance with specified coverage limits.

Provisions defining ownership, licensing, and usage rights for intellectual property including patents, designs, software, documentation, and technical know-how. Specifies IP ownership and license grants.
Maximum financial liability amount that either party can be held responsible for under the sales contract. Limits exposure for damages, losses, or claims arising from contract performance.
Timestamp when this sales contract record was last updated in the system. Part of audit trail for change tracking and compliance.
Net monetary value of the sales contract after applying discounts, rebates, and before taxes. Represents the actual revenue to be recognized.
Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the sales contract. Used for internal communication and knowledge transfer.

Instrument or mechanism through which the customer will remit payment. Wire transfer for electronic bank transfers; letter of credit for trade finance; bank guarantee for secured payments; check for paper-based payments; credit card for card payments; direct debit for automated withdrawals.
Detailed description of payment conditions including milestones, due dates, installment schedules, and payment triggers. Examples: Net 30, 50% upfront 50% on delivery, milestone-based payments.
Contractual provisions defining financial penalties or liquidated damages for non-performance, late delivery, or breach of SLA commitments. Specifies penalty amounts, triggers, and caps.
Provisions governing automatic renewal, renewal options, or renegotiation terms at contract expiration. Defines renewal notice periods, pricing adjustments, and renewal conditions.
Date when the sales contract was signed by both parties and became legally executed. May differ from effective date if contract has a future start date.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Resolution Time Hours
description: Maximum number of hours within which the supplier must resolve customer issues or restore service under the contract. Part of SLA commitments for uptime and availability.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Response Time Hours
description: Maximum number of hours within which the supplier must respond to customer service requests or incidents under the contract. Part of SLA commitments for support and maintenance.
Guaranteed minimum uptime or availability percentage for systems, equipment, or services covered by the sales contract. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.5% uptime).
Full name of the authorized representative who signed the sales contract on behalf of the supplier. Used for legal verification and audit trail.
Total tax amount applicable to the sales contract. Includes VAT, GST, sales tax, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction and product classification.
Contractual provisions defining conditions, notice periods, and procedures under which either party may terminate the sales contract. Includes termination for cause, convenience, or force majeure.
Total monetary value of the sales contract representing the gross revenue commitment. Includes all line items, products, services, and deliverables before adjustments.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract value. Defines the currency in which all financial terms, payments, and penalties are denominated.
Duration in months for which the supplier provides warranty coverage for products, systems, or equipment delivered under the sales contract. Covers defects in materials and workmanship.
Detailed description of warranty provisions including coverage scope, exclusions, repair or replacement terms, and customer obligations. Defines what is covered and what is not under warranty.
Unique identifier for the sales opportunity record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to product.catalog_entry. Business justification: Opportunity qualification in manufacturing CPQ: opportunities are scoped to a specific catalog entry (configurable_flag, list_price, regional_availability, sales_channel) to validate product availabil
Reference to the primary contact person at the customer account for this opportunity.
Reference to the customer account associated with this opportunity.

Reference to the quote that is currently synced with the opportunity amount and line items.
Total estimated revenue value of the opportunity in the base currency. Represents the gross deal size before discounts.
Target date by which the opportunity is expected to close (win or loss decision).
Actual date when the opportunity was marked as closed (won or lost). Null if still open.
Name of the primary competitor identified in this sales opportunity.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the opportunity location.
Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the opportunity amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).
Current status of product delivery and installation for won opportunities. Tracks post-sale fulfillment.
Detailed narrative description of the opportunity including customer needs, solution scope, and strategic context.
Total discount percentage applied to the opportunity amount. Used for pricing and margin analysis.
Probability-weighted revenue calculated as amount multiplied by probability percentage. Used for forecast accuracy.
Fiscal quarter in which the opportunity is expected to close (e.g., Q1 FY2024). Used for quota and forecast alignment.
Fiscal year in which the opportunity is expected to close. Used for annual revenue planning.
Sales forecast classification indicating confidence level for revenue planning and quota tracking.
Boolean indicator whether there are open tasks or activities associated with this opportunity. Used for pipeline health monitoring.
Target industry vertical or market segment for this opportunity (e.g., automotive, food and beverage, pharmaceuticals).

Boolean indicator whether the opportunity is marked as private with restricted visibility.

Date of the most recent sales activity (call, meeting, email) logged against this opportunity.

Original source or channel through which the opportunity was generated.

Business-friendly name or title of the sales opportunity describing the potential deal.
Description of the next planned action or milestone to advance the opportunity.
Externally-visible unique business identifier for the opportunity used in sales communications and reporting.
Classification of the opportunity based on customer relationship status (new customer acquisition vs existing customer expansion).
Reference to the order number generated when the opportunity was converted to a sales order in the ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system.
Estimated probability of winning this opportunity expressed as a percentage (0-100). Used for weighted pipeline forecasting.
Primary product line or business unit category for the opportunity (e.g., automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure).
Planned or actual completion date for project delivery if the opportunity is won.
Planned or actual start date for project delivery if the opportunity is won.
Geographic region classification for the opportunity (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC).
Number of days from opportunity creation to close date. Used for sales velocity analysis.

Timestamp when the opportunity stage was last changed. Used for stage duration and velocity analysis.
